title: Limonium flagellare
intro: Limonium flagellare, also known as sea lavender, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Plumbaginaceae family, commonly found in coastal areas and rocky shores. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Limonium flagellare include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from Limonium flagellare include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat a range of health conditions, including gastrointestinal issues and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Limonium flagellare is classified as a species within the family Plumbaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Limonium and is a type of coastal plant. Limonium flagellare is a type of sea lavender that is commonly found in Mediterranean regions. The classification also places it within the order Plumbaginales and the phylum Angiospermophyta. It is often referred to by its synonym, Staticocarpus flagellare.
uses: Limonium flagellare has been reported to have various medicinal properties, including antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential therapeutic benefits. Research suggests that its extracts may have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects.
